description: Strong junior evaluated against UC Berkeley (highly selective, ~11-15% acceptance). The selectivity floor in fit_computation.py requires the school to land in REACH or SUPER_REACH — never SAFETY/TARGET — regardless of profile strength.
business_rationale: Highly selective public flagships are a high-trust operator surface — many strong students apply expecting realistic guidance. A misadvisement here (e.g. 'UC Berkeley is a TARGET') would erode confidence broadly. This archetype catches floor-rule regression for the 8-15% acceptance band, which today's fit_ultra_selective_reach scenario doesn't exercise (it covers <8%). Together they pin both halves of the selectivity-floor rule.
tests: Selectivity floor (8-15% band): fit_category must be REACH or SUPER_REACH; cannot be SAFETY or TARGET
match_percentage in the band declared by whichever category lands (REACH 35-54 or SUPER_REACH 0-34)
All four factors {Academic, Holistic, Major Fit, Selectivity} present with bounded scores
All 8 advisory blocks present and non-empty
fit_category strictly in {SAFETY, TARGET, REACH, SUPER_REACH}
